notes_from_pm: Lepsius, IV; Perring and Vyse, I of Giza; Reisner, G I; called Great or First Pyramid.
North Subsidiary Pyramid. Lepsius, V; Perring and Vyse, 7 of Giza; Reisner, G I-a.
Middle Subsidiary Pyramid. Lepsius, VI; Perring and Vyse, 8 of Giza; Reisner, G I-b.
South Subsidiary Pyramid. Lepsius, VII; Perring and Vyse, 9 of Giza; Reisner, G I-c. Attributed to Henutsen (wife of Khufu).
Lepsius, VIII; Perring and Vyse, 2 of Giza; Reisner, G II; called Second Pyramid.
SUBSIDIARY PYRAMID WITH ‘SERDAB’. Reisner, G II-a.
Lepsius, IX; Perring and Vyse, 3 of Giza; Reisner, G III; called Third Pyramid.
East Subsidiary Pyramid. Lepsius, XII; Perring and Vyse, 5 of Giza; Reisner, G III-a; sometimes called Fourth Pyramid.
Middle Subsidiary Pyramid. Lepsius, XI; Perring and Vyse, 4 of Giza; Reisner, G III-b.
West Subsidiary Pyramid. Lepsius, X; Perring and Vyse, 6 of Gîza; Reisner, G III-c.

(sometimes called Khnembaf). Hereditary prince, Companion, Overseer of all works of the King, etc. (Probably Vizier of Shepseskaf.) End of Dyn. IV to early Dyn. V. Father (probably), Duaenrēʿ (tomb G 5110).
Not marked on plan. Head of male statuette, Old Kingdom, from shaft A, in Boston Mus. 34.55.

Greatest of the Ten of Upper Egypt, Overseer of all works of the King. Temp. Neferirkarea or later. Stone-built mastaba. LG 31.

Judge and Overseer of scribes, etc. Late Dyn. V or Dyn. VI. Mother (probably), Meresʿankh (on statue of Khenut).
Fragment with names of Ptaḥiufni King's waʿb-priest, Inspector of ka-servants, etc., and wife Hemtrea, Dyn. V-VI, probably from here.
Late Dyn. V or Dyn. VI. Stone-built mastaba. LG 28.
Chief Justice and Vizier, etc. Early Dyn. VI. Wife, Hemtrea Prophetess of Neith Opener-of-the-Ways.
Overseer of the Memphite and Letopolite nomes, Overseer of the new settlements of the Pyramid of Isesi, Inspector of waʿb-priests of the Pyramid of Khufu, etc. Early Dyn. VI.
Steward of the Great Estate, Prophet of Khufu, Saḥurēʿ, Neferirkarēʿ, and Neuserrēʿ, etc. Middle to end of Dyn. V. Parents, Iymery and Nikauḥathor (tomb G 6020). Wife, Khenut Royal acquaintance.
Prophet of Khufu, Steward of the Great Estate, Scribe of the archives, etc. Temp. Neuserrēʿ or later. Father, Shepseskaf-ʿankh (tomb G 6040). Wife, Nikauḥathor Royal acquaintance, etc.

Overseer of singing in the Great House, Prophet of Rēʿ and Ḥathor in the Sun-temple of Neferirkarēʿ, Prophet of Neferirkarēʿ, Saḥurēʿ, and Neuserrēʿ, etc. Middle Dyn. V. Wife, Usertka Royal acquaintance (daughter of Shepseskaf-ʿankh, tomb G 6040).
Dyn. V-VI.
Steward, etc. Temp. Neferirkarēʿ.

TOMB OF HETEPHERES [I]. Temp. Khufu. Husband, Snefru. Son, Khufu. No superstructure. Re-burial, transferred from unidentified tomb probably at Dahshûr. Reisner Excavation. Harvard-Boston Expedition (1925-7).
Probably NEFERTKAU (woman). Temp. Khephren. Father, Snefru. Stone-built mastaba. No inscriptions found.
NEFERMAaET King's son, Hereditary prince, Overlord of Nekheb, etc. (Vizier of Khephren.) Temp. Khufu to Khephren. Mother, Nefertkau (tomb, probably, G 7050). Stone-built mastaba. LG 57.
SNEFRUKHAaEF Treasurer of the King of Lower Egypt, Herdsman of Apis, etc. Middle Dyn. IV to early Dyn. V. Father, Nefermaaet (tomb G 7060). Stone-built mastaba. LG 56.

MERYREaNUFER good name qAR Overseer of the Pyramid-towns of Khufu and Menkaurea, Inspector of waab-priests of the Pyramid of Khephren, Tenant of the Pyramid of Pepy I, etc. Temp. Pepy I or later. Wife, Gefi, Prophetess of Hathor, etc. Stone-built mastaba.

IDU Tenant of the Pyramid of Pepy I, Overseer of scribes of the meret, Inspector of wad-priests of the Pyramids of Khufu and Khephren, etc. Temp. Pepy I or later. Parents (possibly), Meryreanufer Kar and Gefi (tomb G 7101). Wife, Mertiotes. Stone-built mastaba.

G 7110+7120. KAWAaB King's eldest son of his body, Hereditary prince, Chief lector-priest, etc., and wife HETEPHERES [11] (later, wife of King Raazedef). Temp. Khufu. Father of Hetepheres [11], Khufu. Stone-built twin-mastaba. See also tomb G 7350.

G 7110+7120. KAWAaB King's eldest son of his body, Hereditary prince, Chief lector-priest, etc., and wife HETEPHERES [11] (later, wife of King Raazedef). Temp. Khufu. Parents of Kawaab, Khufu and Mertiotes [I] (eldest son). Stone-built twin-mastaba. See also tomb G 7350.
G 7130+7140. KHUFUKHAaEF [I] King's son of his body, Chief Justice and Vizier (probably of Khufu), etc., and wife NEFERTKAU. Temp. Khufu to end of Dyn. IV. Stone-built twin-mastaba.

G 7130+7140. KHUFUKHAaEF [I] King's son of his body, Chief Justice and Vizier (probably of Khufu), etc., and wife NEFERTKAU. Temp. Khufu to end of Dyn. IV. Parents of Khufukhaaef [I], Khufu and probably Henutsen. Stone-built twin-mastaba.
KHUFUKHAaEF [11] Greatest of the Ten of Upper Egypt, Belonging to the foremost seat, Secretary of his Lord. Temp. Neuserrea. Stone-built mastaba.
Chief Justice and Vizier, etc. Late Dyn. V or Dyn. VI.
King's son of his body, Count, Keeper of Nekhen, etc., and his wife. Temp. Khufu to late Dyn. IV. Parents, Khufu and probably Mertiotes [I].
Prophet of Rēʿ in the Sun-temple of Menkauhor, Prophet of the Pyramid of Menkauhor, Inspector of administrators of the Treasury, etc. Late Dyn. V or Dyn. VI.
West wall: false-door of deceased; name and titles on drum. Shaft G 7220A with two rooms. Sarcophagus (unfinished) of Ḥarzedef, red granite, in Cairo Mus. Ent. 54938.
Keeper of the legs of the Great House.
False-door of Mesthu ka-servant, and wife Nubḥotp, Dyn. V-VI, from shaft A, in Boston Mus. 27.1117.
Dyn. IV-V. Box-sealing with Horus-name of Neuserrēʿ, from shaft A.
Middle or late Dyn. IV. Sarcophagus (uninscribed) with panther-skin in relief on lid, and palace-façade decoration, from shaft of G 7340, in Cairo Mus. Ent. 54934.
End of Dyn. IV. Husbands, Kawaab (tomb G 7120) and later King Raʿzedef. See also tomb G 7110.
Inspector of waʿb-priests of the Pyramid of Khephren, Inspector of the Great House, etc. Late Dyn. V. Wife, Sentʿankh Royal acquaintance.

King's daughter of his body, King's wife, etc., and probably Ḥarbaf King's son. Temp. Khufu to early Dyn. V. Parents of Meresʿankh [II], Khufu and probably Mertiotes [I]. Father of Ḥarbaf, probably Khufu.
Dyn. V. Wife, Ḥatḥornufer.

Probably Ḥarbaf. Chapel: relief-fragments with estates, in Boston Mus. Shaft G 7420A: sarcophagus of Ḥarbaf, red granite, probably from here, in Cairo Mus. 1788. Shaft G 7420B: small nude female statuette, wood, Dyn. VI, intrusive.
King's son of his body, Chief Justice and Vizier (of Khephren), etc. Temp. Khufu to Khephren. Parents, Khufu and possibly Henutsen.
Fragment with remains of incised date ('Union of the Two Lands'), from débris.
Mummy-mask, plaster, from burial chamber of shaft B, in Cairo Mus. Ent. 54942.
(Vizier of Khephren.) Temp. Khephren. Parents, Snefru and a minor Queen. Wife, Ḥetepḥeres (eldest daughter of Ḥetepḥeres [I] (tomb G 7000X) and Snefru). No burial place for wife.
Four canopic-jars of Haremakhet son of Hedebiru (woman), Dyn. XXVI, from shaft A, in Boston Mus. 29.1133-6.
King's daughter of his body, etc. Temp. Khufu to Shepseskaf. Parents, Kawaab (tomb G 7120) and Ḥetepḥeres [II] (tombs G 7110, 7350). Husband, Khephren.
